changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_audit_logging_configured for clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security features relevant to system monitoring. Audit logging and admission controllers are appropriate mechanisms for monitoring and enforcing security policies. Pod Security Standards ensure compliance with security configurations. NetworkPolicies can indeed be used for monitoring traffic, but the naming could be more specific. Adjustments were made to improve clarity and specificity in naming.
naming_improvements: Use 'configured' instead of 'enabled' for audit logging to indicate setup completeness
Specify 'traffic' in network policy monitoring to clarify the monitoring aspect
title: System Monitoring - System monitoring | host - based devices implement the following host-based moni
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4(23) focuses on system monitoring using host-based devices, which can be mapped to Kubernetes through several mechanisms. Kubernetes audit logging provides detailed monitoring of API server interactions, which is akin to host-based monitoring. Admission controllers can enforce security policies that ensure pods and workloads adhere to security standards, providing a form of monitoring and control. Pod Security Standards can enforce baseline security configurations, ensuring that pods are monitored for compliance with security policies. NetworkPolicies can be configured to monitor and control network traffic, providing visibility and control over communications within the cluster.
